entry.title: 入室行窃
entry.content:
Mr Liu, a mandarin speaking man, is reporting a home burglary to the police.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hi. Mr. Liu. Now the interpreter is here. We are able to understand you now You have reported that you’ve been robbed; please tell me when and what did they take.31
M1谢天谢地，你终于来了。今天晚上我回家的时候发现门是开着的，我进去一 看，有个人正在往我新买的宝马上装我那心爱的 60 寸电视。Thank goodness you are finally here. When I came home tonight I found my door was unlocked. When I got in closer, I saw a guy was loading my treasured 60’’ TV into my newly bought BMW.
E2Did you get a good look at the guy who was trying to take your stuff， and did you confront him?22
M2没看清楚，因为我下班回来已经天黑了，而且那小偷也每开灯。我没敢惹那 小偷，他看到我以后就马上开走了。No, as it was already dark then and the thief did not turn on the light. I didn’t dare confront him. He drove away quickly after he saw me.
E3How many guys did you see at that particular moment? And can you give me a detailed list of things that are missing?23
M3我隐隐约约看到车里有 2 个人，加上房间里的。一共 3 个。丢的东西太多 了，你让我好好看看房间里少了什么，因为我刚刚一直在等你们，没敢碰里 面的东西。I vaguely saw there were two guys in the car, plus the one inside my house, so in total there were three. I lost so many things. Please let me have a look at what has been stolen because I have been waiting for you and did not touch anything.
E4You are right to keep the crime scene uncontaminated. Ok, Mr. Liu, now you can go in and see what’s missing. Just be careful, try not to touch anything on the floor or on the walls and doors.38
M4恩。刚买的车被开走了，丢的还有电视，电脑，游戏机，iPad。还有一些 钱。Hmm, the car I just bought was driven away. My TV, computer, video game console, iPad and some cash were also stolen.
E5I think the cash is something you won’t be able to claim. Can you give me an estimated total value of the stuff missing, and have you got home and contents insurance?32
M5所有丢的东西加起来一共$5 万多块钱吧，车买了保险，其他的没有保险，因 为是租的房子怎么可能有保险。That would be around $50,000 in total. The car is insured but the others are not. It’s impossible for me to get insurance for this is only a rented place.
E6Ok, I will give you a police note with which you can make a claim for the car from your insurer, but as for the other things, you didn’t get them insured so there is nothing I can do.39
M6好吧，那也只能干认倒霉吧。希望你们能尽快把这些窃贼捉拿归案。谢谢你 了警官。All right, it seems I have to take this bad luck. I hope you can catch those burglars as soon as possible. Thank you officer.

entry.title: 入室砸抢
entry.content:
This is a conversation between Superintendent Watson and Mr Wu, who has just experienced a home burglary.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello Mr Wu, now we have an interpreter with us and we can understand each other. So please tell me what happened.22
M1你好警官，是这样的，我今天早上一起来，发现我家里被人进来过了。我的 电视机被人碎了，我才刚买 2 天啊，56 寸的最新 LED 电视就这么没了。你 一定要帮我找到这个混蛋啊。Hello officer. This is what happened: when I woke up this morning, I found somebody had broken into my house. My TV was smashed. I’d just bought this latest 56’’ TV two days ago but now it’s broken! Please help me find the bastards!
E2Well. Please tell me more details. Otherwise I’m afraid we won’t be able to help you. Please tell me when exactly you noticed you’d been burgled, and is there any other damage?31
M2我是早上 8 点起来，要去厕所刷牙，可当我路过客厅的时候发现有什么不对 劲。当我看见我新买的电视就这么被人砸了，我差点没晕倒。哪个混蛋做的 啊！除了电视，我发现我新买的 Ipad mini 还有所配套的配件都被人卷跑了。I got up at 8 am and I found something strange when I walked through the living room as I was going to the bathroom to brush my teeth. The view of my new TV smashed almost made me faint. Besides the TV, my new iPad Mini and its accessories have all been stolen too.
E3Well. That’s terrible. You have my sympathy. However, it seems like the burglar was targeting at your new home entertainment system. Does anyone know that youhave recently bought those things?31
M3我想很多人应该都知道吧。我一买回来安装好就拍照片上传到各种社交网络 上了，比如说推特，脸书还有微博。我当时很开心，就想和亲戚朋友分享一 下快乐，没想到这居然引来了犯罪。真是太倒霉了。I think many people know about it. I set everything up as soon as I brought them home, then I uploaded photos to social media including Twitter, Facebook and Weibo. I was happy and I wanted to share this happiness with my friends and relatives. I didn’t expect it would entice a crime. What bad luck!
E4Well. Then I believe there must be some sort of association between this and your posts on your social networking sites. We have noticed some new crimes, which are a result of people mistakenly showing their location information and wealth.38
M4是么。我没想到这种事情会发生在我身上。我并不知道我发送的图片会包涵 了我的地理信息。不过算了，现在我打算把那些东西都关掉了。你能帮我查 查么，录入指纹什么的，搞不好就是住在周围的哪个小毛贼干的。Yes. I never thought this would happen to me. I didn’t know the photos I posted included my location. But never mind. I’m going to turn off these functions. Could you help me check things, maybe take fingerprints or something? It might have be done by a burglar who lives around my area.
E5Sure, our constable is recording fingerprints now. Please be patient and we will give you a report when we finish. Could you please give me your photo ID? We want to keep it on record.36
M5好的，给你，这是我的驾照。请有任何消息第一时间通知我。谢谢。Okay, here you are. This is my driver’s licence. Please inform me immediately if anything comes up. Thank you.

entry.title: 误会贩毒
entry.content:
Mrs Wang is stopped by a customs officer, who suspects she is smuggling drugs.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1喂，你们为什么把我带到这里啊，我的飞机马上就要起飞了，我错过飞机谁负责？Hey, why have you brought me here? My plane is going to take off soon. Who’s going to take responsibility for me missing the plane?
M1Ma’am, please calm down. The reason we brought you here was because we have reasons to believe that you were trying to smuggle drugs out of the country.28
E2什么！这不可能，我连烟都不抽，怎么可能碰毒品呀。你们一定弄错了。What? That’s impossible! I don’t even touch cigarettes, let alone drugs. You must have made a mistake!
M2According to your boarding pass, your name is Wang Chen, is that correct? And is this your bag?18
E3对，我叫王晨，是我的包呀。但是看着有点别扭，怎么好像被打开过，因为 我记得我的锁不是这样的。Yes, I am Wang Chen and this is my bag. But it looks a bit odd and as if it has been opened, because I remember my lock was not like this.
M3Really? You actually think that I will believe that? Did you pack your bag or did someone else do it for you? Or did someone give you this bag?29
E4我自己装的包啊，里面是一些巧克力，是带给我女儿的。我不可能给我女儿带毒品吧。你们肯定搞错了。不信我打开给你看。It was me who packed the bag. There are some chocolates for my daughter in there. It would be insane if I brought my daughter drugs. You must be wrong. I can open it if you don’t believe me.
M4Please go ahead. We will need to examine the contents of this bag; we have been informed that you are an international criminal, and we believe there is cocaine in it.31
E5放屁，我是来旅游的，我一直都是跟旅游团在一起，而且我一直是一个遵纪守法的人，怎么可能是罪犯。包打开了，你们自己看，里面都是什么。Bullshit! I’m here for a trip and I stayed with the group all the time. Also I’ve been always a law-abiding person and there is no way I’m a criminal. Now I’ve opened the bag. You can check what’s in it.
M5We are just following procedure; we need to go through the bag. Let me just ask you, did you lend the bag to anyone while you were in Australia?29
E6借给过我们一个旅游团的人，他说他东西多借我的包装下东西，我没多想就借给他了。这有什么问题嘛?I lent it to another guy in our group. He said he had too much stuff and wanted to borrow my bag to put something in. I did not think anything of it before I lent it to him. Is there any problem?
M6Yes, we detected traces of cocaine on the bag. And we were of the belief that you were trying to take drugs out of the country. But it is clear that we have made a mistake. Sorry, madam, you can catch your flight now.44

entry.title: 耳鸣就医
entry.content:
Ms Wang is having a consultation with an ENT Doctor, about treating her blocked ear.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1医生，我右边耳朵堵了，现在听声音很费劲。有时候还耳鸣。Doctor, my right ear is blocked and I have trouble hearing. Also, sometimes my ears are ringing.
M1I see. Firstly, could you tell me how long you’ve had this problem? Has it happened before? Are you in any pain?22
E2这是第一次，两天前开始的。 目前还不痛，可是听得不是很清楚，好像耳朵被人蒙着那样，听到的声音都变得很低沉。 这种感觉在人多、嘈杂的地方就更加明显。This is the first time and it stared two days ago. I feel no pain yet, but I can’t hear clearly. It’s as if my ear is covered and everything sounds so low and deep. This feeling becomes worse when I am at noisy, crowed places.
M2I see. Let me have a look at this one first. Umm, it’s inflamed. And the right one is completely blocked by wax too. And it seems the wax has been there for quite a long time. Do you clean your ears regularly?43
E3原来是耳屎。我还担心自己得了中耳炎， 听说中耳炎也会有类似的症状。我基本不经常掏耳屎， 因为我一直都是油耳，不是康耳。 耳朵堵住之前，我侧躺着看电视看了一天。It’s the wax? I thought I had otitis media, as I heard the symptoms are similar. I don’t clean my ears very often, because I’ve always had oily ears not dry ones. Before my ear got blocked, I was lying on my side and watching TV for a whole day.
M3That might be the cause. We have to put eardrops in. This is to soften the wax so that it can be removed. We can’t do it now while it is still hard.33
E4滴耳药水？我从来没有滴过, 去会疼吗？我直接去药房买就可以了吧？ 需要您开一张处方给我吗？Ear drops? I have never put ear drops in before. Does it hurt? Can I get them from the chemist straightaway or do I need a prescription from you?
M4If the wax keeps building up it causes too much pressure. It may cause damage to the ear drum and then impair your hearing. This procedure is common. It won’t be painful at all. It is a non-prescriptive medicine, so you can get it easily from any chemist.48
E5说实话，我挺讨厌往耳朵里滴药水。 光想想我就觉得有点毛骨悚然。一定要这样做吗？还有别的办法吗？To be honest, I hate putting drops into my ears. It makes me creep all over just at the thought of doing so. Do I have to do this? Are there any other solutions?
M5I’m afraid not. This is quite important for your hearing. You really must do this. Apply about five drops in both ears and leave it for ten to thirty minutes. Do that for a couple of days and come back to see me.43
E6请问滴了这个药水之后， 我是不是该用小棉塞堵住耳朵， 不让药水流出来？之后要怎样才能把耳屎清出来呢？After applying, should I put a small cotton plug into my ear to stop the liquid from running out? So how is the wax removed after that?
M6Yes. It depends on how severe the ear is impacted. In some cases, the loosened ear wax may be cleared from the ear itself. In more severe cases, we may need to remove it with a syringe.37

entry.title: 儿子打架
entry.content:
This is a conversation between Mr. Craig, a school teacher and a parent Mr Wan, whose son was involved in a fight.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello Mrs. Wan. Thanks for coming on time today. I am going to talk with you about your son James.20
M1你好老师，没事儿，儿子的事儿才是最重要的事儿。他出什么事了？Hello, Mr. Craig. Not a problem at all. Nothing is more important to me than my son. What happened?
E2He has always been a good student at school. But this morning, he fought with another student and injured that student’s face. Do you know anything about that?28
M2啊？不会吧？这不可能。他一向都非常乖，无论是在家还是在学校。怎么会 跟人打架呢。一定是你搞错了。What? Really? No Way. He has been so well-behaved all the time, both at school and at home. My son would never get involved in a fight. It must be a mistake.
E3Unfortunately, I am not joking with you. He did severely hurt the other boy. At first, I couldn’t believe it either, but when I went to the school clinic I found out it was true.35
M3啊！那真是对不起。现在 James 在哪里？有没有受伤？那另一个小孩现在怎 样了，对方家长说什么了么？Really? I’m sorry. Where is James now? Is he injured? How is the other boy? Have his parents said anything?
E4James is fine and is now talking to our school psychologist. We think he might have some unstable psychological condition. By the way, the other boy’s parents are very angry and they are at the hospital now.37
M4这一切真的是太意外了。James 性格内向，一向很听话， 甚至都很少和人争 执。您知道他们到底是因为什么才打架的吗？This is so shocking! James is an introverted and obedient child. He rarely argues with others. Do you know what on earth caused this fight?
E5So far, we are not so clear about the reason they fought, but I was told by some students in his class that it’s because of a girl.28
M5因为一个女孩？现在的小孩怎么那么早熟啊。而且他才是一个小学生，懂什么叫做爱情啊。我看他是应该去看看心理医生了。我现在都不知道怎么去面 对另外一个孩子的家长了。Because of a girl? How come today’s children get mature so early? Besides, he is just a primary school student, what does he know about love? I think it is time for him to see a psychologist. I don’t know how to face the other kid’s parents.
E6I’d suggest you talk to your son and find out what is going on with him. And go to the hospital to apologise to the other boy’s parents. I don’t think they will take you to the court if you sincerely apologise to them.44
M6好的。我知道了，我现在就去校医室看看我的儿子。然后我会带他一起去医 院给对方赔礼道歉去。谢谢你了老师，给你们添大麻烦了。All right, I see. I’ll go to the clinic to see my son right now. Then I will take him to the hospital and make a sincere apology to the other boy’s family. Sorry for the trouble we caused you.

entry.title: 母亲痴呆
entry.content:
Mr. Lu, a new Chinese immigrant in Australia, is talking to Joe, an ACAT (Aged Care Assessment Team) consultant, about placing his mother into an aged care centre.

        
        
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good Morning, Mr. Lu. Thanks for coming in today. How can I help you?14
M1早上好，我最近在考虑帮我的母亲找一个合适的养老院，所以来咨询一下你。Good morning, I’ve been considering finding my mother a good aged care centre. So I am here to make some enquiries.
E2Certainly, in order to assist you better, could you give me some details on the applicant before we talk through the admission procedure?23
M2好的。我母亲得了老年痴呆症。一开始只是记忆力不好，比如说， 有一次她独自上街却忘记家在哪里了。还有就是，最近我觉得和她 沟通越来越困难了。Sure. My mother has got senile dementia. At first, it was just that her memory wasn’t very good. For example, one day she went out on her own but later forgot where she lived. And I’ve found it’s getting more and more difficult to communicate with her recently.
E3Well, I am sorry to hear that. It is quite normal for many of us to think we need to place our parents in residential care places when they have a condition that is severe or likely to worsen. // But it is not the only option. Often, people who are highly dependent often manage quite well at home with the help of a carer, family, friends or community services.70
M3我一开始也打算在家自己照顾她，但其实主要是我太太在照顾，我 工作比较忙。更糟糕的是，最近我母亲老是莫名其妙的和我太太起 争执。前几天洗澡的时候她摔断了胳膊，当时我就觉得要把她送老 人院了。I originally planned to look after her at home. Well, actually my wife is the main carer as I am very busy at work. But what’s worse, my mother started to quarrel with my wife a lot for no reason. And when she broke her arm in the shower the other day, I realised it’s time to place her in aged care.
E4I see. Well, there are currently a variety of aged care services offered in the community. I’m sure we can work together with you, and find the best arrangement for your mother. First, your mother will need to be assessed so we can decide what kind of service is best for her.52
M4恩，这没问题。但我想了解一下，这个评估是由谁来做的呢？评估 的流程又是怎样的呢？That’s not a problem. But I’d like to know who will be the assessors and what the assessment procedure is like.
E5The assessment is run by one or two members of the aged care assessment team or ACAT. They will visit your place and discuss with you whether community help will enable your mother to continue living at home or whether her needs would be better met through our aged care home.51
M5那你能告诉我，评估的时候通常会问哪些问题呢？这样我好提前准 备一些相关的书面材料给评估人员看。Could you tell me what sorts of questions will be asked during the assessment? So that I can prepare some documents in advance to provide to the assessors.
E6Questions asked at each assessment vary as they pertain to the individual, but they often include the types of daily living activities your mother needs help with, her general state of health and any other health issues she might have.40
M6我朋友说这边的养老院挺好的。但是我妈妈比较挑剔，所以希望您 帮忙找个好一点的，这样她就不会搬来搬去。I heard from my friend that the aged care services here are quite nice. But my mother is a bit picky, so I hope you can help her find a good place to avoid having to move her again in the future.
E7Well, Mr. Lu, I suggest that you fill out this application form first and we will see what results come from the assessment. Then we can make any further arrangements, Okay?31
M7好的，谢谢，我注意到外面的架子上有很多小册子，我可以去拿几 本回家看看吗？Sure, thank you. I noticed there are many brochures on the shelf outside, can I take some to have a read at home？

entry.title: 产后忧郁
entry.content:
Dr. Bride is in a conversation with a patient, Mrs. Yu, about inviting her to join his research on post-natal depression.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Hello, Mrs. Yu. Good to see you today. How are you feeling?12
M1你好医生，我很好，谢谢。待会儿我准备和我先生去看场电影。Hello Doctor, I am very well. Thank you. I am going to see a movie with my husband later on.
E2That’s great. Your file indicates that you were diagnosed with post-natal depression early last month and have undergone treatment. It seems you are a lot better now, right?28
M2是的。记得我第一次来看您的时候，状况真的糟透了。我当时真的觉 得人生没有喜乐，很无助。要不是我先生逼我来看医生，我可能现在 还在痛苦中挣扎。Yes. I remember when I first came to see you, my condition couldn’t be worse. I found no joy in my life and I felt helpless. I would still be suffering right now if my husband hadn’t urged me to see a doctor.
E3I’m happy to hear that you’re making progress, Mrs Yu. I’d like to let you know that we are currently doing research on post-natal depression and we’d like to invite you to be part of this research.37
M3关于产后抑郁的研究，到底是个什么研究呢？我并不介意加入你们的 研究，不过你能向我简单介绍一下这项研究吗？Research on post-natal depression? What is that exactly? I don’t mind being part of it, but could you tell me a bit more about it?
E4The research is called “The ABX Discovery Program”. We’re now at Phase 2 of the program which involves clinical trials of a new drug ABX on patients with post-natal depression.30
M4就是说要我帮您试药是吗？这对我和胎儿来说，安全吗？我现在还在 吃你上一次给我开的药呢。You mean you want me to try out this new medication for you? Is it safe for me and the baby? I’m now still taking the medication you prescribed for me last time.
E5ABX is a new anti-depressant. It has been tested before with healthy volunteers and presented some very positive results. We now want to test the drug on patients with post-natal depression to find out if the new drug would work better than the drug you’re currently taking.47
M5我的另外一个担心是， 这药还没正式获得政府批准， 会不会有副作 用？万一出现了意料之外的状况怎么办？I have another concern. Since this medication is not approved by the government yet, could there be some side effects? What if something happens out of the blue?
E6This drug has gone through a number of laboratory tests, including tests on humans. As I indicated earlier, we know of no toxins or negative side effects at this moment. But if it is proven effective through our research program, it may help more women with similar cases to you.50
M6那我还是先回家和我先生商量一下再做决定吧。 毕竟还是有风险的， 对吧？所以，您也别抱太大希望了。I think I should go back and talk with my husband about it before making any decisions. After all, it still involves some risks, right? So don’t get your hopes up.

entry.title: 移民专业
entry.content:
This is a dialogue between Jason, a migration agent (MA) and Mr. Wang, a recently graduated international student.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning, Mr. Wang. My name is Jason, and I’m a migration agent. I understand that you came here because your student visa is going to expire shortly. Is that right?31
M1是的，你好。我非常担心，我已经毕业了 2 个月了。下个月我的学生签证就 过期了。我想一直留在这里工作生活，但是我学的专业不能移民，现在也找 不到工作。我可不想这么快就回去。Yes, I’m very worried. I graduated two months ago and my student visa is expiring next month. I’ve always wanted to stay here. But I don’t qualify for immigration because of my major and I can’t find a job now. I don’t want to go back home so soon.
E2Don’t worry, Mr. Wang. I’m here to help you with this. But firstly, please tell me more about your education background and the exact date of expiry of your student visa.31
M2希望你们真的能帮到我。我是 2010 年 2 月 24 号持 573 学生签证来澳洲读书 的，我在 UNSW 学建筑学的本科。I hope you can help me out. I came to Australia for study on a 573 visa on 24th February 2010. I did a Bachelor of Architecture at the University of NSW.
E3Umm … Let me have a look at your file. Yes, your student visa is going to expire next month, and you are not entitled to apply for a Skilled GraduatesTemporary Visa, since your occupation is not on the Skilled Occupation List.43
M3我来澳洲的时候我的专业还是在这个列表里的，但是去年 7 月份这个职业就 不再是移民专业了。所以我现在处境很艰难，而且我现在也很难找到工作申 请工作签证。我还能怎么办呢？My occupation was on the list when I arrived but it hasn’t been since July last year. So I’m in a very difficult situation. Besides, it’s very hard for me to get a job to apply for a working visa. What else can I do?
E4My suggestion is that you apply for another course which matches the SOL. For example, you can apply for a TAFE course closely related to your occupation such as welding, bricklaying, tiling and so on. Then you may have a better chance to secure permanent employment as well as to apply for your permanent residency after you graduate.58
M4啊？这些在中国都是低等且没前途的工作，干这个会给人笑的。而且这些工 作干一辈子也赚不了多少钱啊，我父母是不会同意的。What? In China, these are all considered inferior jobs with no real future prospects. I will be looked down upon if I pick on of those professions. Also, even if I do this job my life, I won’t earn much. My parents will never agree.
E5Mr Wang. You shouldn’t be biased towards these occupations. In Australia, every occupation is treated equally. Occupations that I have mentioned above are all on the SOL, and you can earn a considerable income if you have one of those jobs. Most importantly, you can easily get a permanent resident visa under any GSM Scheme or Employer Nomination Scheme.59
M5是这样啊，那我知道了。那未来几年移民政策还会变么？这些职业该不会很 快又不在列表上了吧。All right, I understand. Will there be other changes to the immigration policy in the near future? Is it possible that those occupations will be removed from the list soon?
E6At this stage, I can tell you all these occupations are still in high demand. However, I cannot guarantee no change will occur, because the SOL is subject to a series of variations. We are not responsible for any future changes to the list.44
M6这就是我担心的。我第一次来的时候我也以为我的专业在这个国家，尤其是 偏远地区还是很急缺的。谁知后来说变就变了。算了，我还是再去别家中介 问问看吧。That’s my concern. When I first came here I thought my occupation was in high demand, especially in remote areas. Who could predict the sudden change that followed? Never mind, I’d better go to some other agents and make some enquiries.

entry.title: 腿肿就医
entry.content:
The following is a dialogue between an orthopaedic specialist (S) and Mr. Tian (T), who accidently fell and injured his right leg.
IDOriginalTranslated
E1Good morning Mr. Tian. I’ve examined the X-ray image. There is no obvious damage to the bone in your right leg.21
M1那我就放心一点了。我还以为我摔断了腿呢，因为实在是疼痛难忍。 而且，腿已经肿了有十天了，可把我吓坏了。I am a little bit relieved now as I was thinking that I’d broken my leg as the pain was unbearable. Also, the swelling persisted for ten days and I was really terrified.
E2As an orthopaedic specialist, I can tell you that the swelling was due to soft tissue damage, and physiotherapy sessions would hugely benefit your recovery.25
M2我听说那个物理治疗很贵的。 况且， 我也不方便经常去那么远的地 方。您能不能就只是给我开点药呢？I heard that physiotherapy is rather expensive. Also, it’s really inconvenient for me to travel this far on a regular basis. Can you prescribe some drugs instead?
E3Actually Mr. Tian, regular physical treatment such as massage, hydrotherapy and physical exercise would all be much more beneficial and effective than drugs or surgery.25
M3确实如此。可是，我还是需要先跟我的家人商量一下，才能给你一个 确切的回复。我这两天跟他们说了之后，再告诉你。That’s so true… nevertheless, I’ll still need to discuss this matter with my family before I can give you a definite answer. I’ll let you know within a couple of days after I’ve done that.
E4That’s fine. But you know that the sooner you start treatment, the better your chances of a full recovery are. But please know that the related costs are not covered by Medicare.31
M4这可真成问题了。我贷款盖了房子，可是房贷还没有付完，我也没有 足够多的存款啊。我怎么能负担得起这么长期的治疗呢？That is a real problem. I haven’t paid off my home loan that I took while building my house and I don’t have enough savings either. How can I afford such lengthy treatment now?
E5Are you covered by any private health insurance? You just have to work out a plan to focus on your recovery. After all you are still a young man and you have a bright future.35
M5我目前还没有。您能不能帮我查一下，我是否能获得某种治疗费用上 的优惠？或者，我能分期付款吗？I don’t have one at the moment. Can you please check whether I could get some sort of concession for the treatment? Or can I pay the fees in instalments?
E6I’ll talk to the director of the financial department about this issue. In my opinion, they may well consider your financial position and give you some discount.27
M6那太谢谢您了。非常感激。Thank you very much for that. Truly appreciated.

entry.title: RA朗读原文
entry.content:
连读断句失爆

150: Soil Samples [ 新题 ]

        
        
Investigators also compared those microbes with those living in 52 other soil samples taken from all around the planet. The park had organisms that also exist in deserts, frozen tundra, forests, rainforests, and prairies. Antarctica was the only area that had microbes that did not overlap with those found in Central Park. Only a small percentage of the park’s microbes were found to be already listed in databases.

112 MBA
Exhilarating, exhausting and intense. There are just some of the words used to describe doing an MBA. Everyone’s experience of doing MBA is, of course, different through denying that it’s hard and a demanding work whichever course you do. MBA is one of the fastest growing areas of studying in the UK so that must be a sustainable benefit against form in one pain.

124: Australian English [ 04/12/19 更新 ]
Australians speak English of course. But for many tourists and even some locals, Australian English has only tenuous links with the mother tongue. Our speech is peppered with words and phrases whose arcane meanings are understood only by the native speaker. It is these colorful colloquialisms that Australian slang is yet to truly explain. 

1: Blue 
While blue is one of the most popular colors, it is one of the least appetizing. Food researchers say that when humans searched for food, they learned to avoid toxic or spoiled objects, which were often blue, black or purple. When food dyed blue is served to study subjects, they lose appetite.

3: Tesla and Edison 
Tesla’s theoretical work formed the basis of modern alternating current electric power systems. Thomas Edison promised him almost one million dollars in today’s money to undertake motor and generator improvement. However, when Tesla, the ethical Serb, asked about the money, Edison’s reported reply was”Tesla, you don’t understand our American humor.” The pair became archrivals.

4: Productive Capacity 
        
            
        
        
The core of the problem / was the immense disparity / between the country’s productive capacity / and the ability of people to consume. Great innovations_in productive techniques / during and after the war / raised the output_of industry / beyond the purchasing capacity / of U.S. farmers and wage earners.

 
5: Father [ 修订 ] 
Every morning, no matter how late he had been up, my father rose at five-thirty, went to his study, wrote for a couple of hours, made us all breakfast, read the paper with my mother, and then went back to work for the rest of the morning. Many years passed before I realized that he did this by choice, for a living, and that he was not unemployed or mentally ill.

7: Pluto 
        
            
        
        
Pluto lost_its official status / as_a planet yesterday, when the International Astronomical Union downsized the solar system / from nine to eight planets. Although there had been_a passionate debate / at the IAU General Assembly Meeting in Prague / about the definition of_a planet - and whether Pluto met the specifications - the audience greeted the decision / to exclude_it with_applause.

8: Fiscal Year 
        
            
        
        
At the beginning of each fiscal year, funds_are allocated to each State_account / in_accordance with the University’s financial plan. Funds_are allocated to each_account / by objects of expenditure. Account managers are responsible for ensuring that / adequate funds are available in the appropriate object / before initiating transactions to use the funds.

9: Lincoln 
Lincoln’s apparently radical change of mind about his war power to emancipate slaves was caused by the escalating scope of the war, which convinced him that any measure to weaken the Confederacy and strengthen the Union war effort was justifiable as a military necessity.

10: Shakespeare 
A young man from a small provincial town – a man without independent wealth, without powerful family connections and without a university education – moves to London in the late 1580’s and, in a remarkably short time, becomes the greatest playwright not of his age alone but of all time. How was this achievement of magnitude made? How does Shakespeare become Shakespeare?

012: Akimbo 
Akimbo, this must be one of the odder-looking words in the language and puzzles us in part because it doesn’t seem to have any relatives, What’s more, it is now virtually a fossil word, until recently almost invariably found in arms akimbo, a posture in which a person stands with hands on hips and elbows sharply bent outward, one signalling impatience or hostility.

13: Yellow 
Yellow is considered as the most optimistic color, yet surprisingly, people lose their tempers most often in yellow rooms and babies will cry more. The reason may be that yellow is the hardest color for eyes to take in, so it can be overpowering if overused.

14: Elephant 
        
            
        
        
The elephant_is the largest living land mammal. During evolution, its skeleton has greatly altered / from the usual mammal, designed for two main reasons. One_is to cope with the great weight / of huge grinding cheek teeth / and elongated tusk, making the skull particularly massive. The other is to support the enormous bulk / of such_a huge body.

16: Yellow Tulip 
        
            
        
        
How do we imagine the unimaginable / if we’re asked to think_of_an_object - say, a yellow tulip - a picture immediately forms / in_our mind’s eye. But what_if we try to imagine a concept / such_as the square root / of_a negative number?

17: Grand Canyon 
Few things in the world produce such amazement as one’s first glimpse of the Grand Canyon; it took around more than 2 billion years to create this vast wonder in some places. 17 miles wide, largely through the relentless force of the Colorado River, which runs 277 miles along its length and a mile beneath its towering rims.

78: Brain Hemispheres [V2] 
修订高频预测:06/2020
The brain is divided into its hemispheres by a prominent groove. At the base of this lies nerve fibers which enable these two halves of the brain to communicate with each other. But the left hemisphere usually controls movement and sensation in the right side of the body, while the right hemisphere similarly controls the left side of the body.

82: Fast Radio Burst 
First discovered in 2007, “fast radio burst” continue to defy explanation. These cosmic chirps last a thousandth of a second. The characteristics of the radio pulses suggested that they came from galaxies billions of light-years away. However, new works points to a much closer origin-flaring star within our own galaxy.

98: Incentive Pay Schemes 
        
            
        
        
If bonus_or “incentive pay” schemes / work so well for senior executives_and bankers, why dose everyone not get them? After all, many jobs involve making important decisions / or taking risks. Is there anything about corporate decisions / and financial risks / that makes these categories_of work special / in terms_of how they need to be incentivised and rewarded?

99: Population Growth
        
            
        
        
How quickly is the world’s population growing? In the United States and other developed countries, the current growth rate is very low. In most developing countries, the human population / is growing at_a rate_of nearly 3 people per second. Because_of this bustling growth rate, the human population is well_on_its way / to reaching 9 billion within lifetime.

101: Augustus 
        
            
        
        
Augustus was given the powers_of_an_absolute monarch, but he presented himself / as the preserver of republican traditions. He treated the Senate, or state council, with great respect, and was made Consul year after year. He successfully reduced the political power of the army / by retiring many soldiers, but giving them land or money / to keep their loyalty.

102: Industrial Revolution 
        
            
        
        
As to the Industrial Revolution, one cannot dispute today / the fact that it has succeeded / in_inaugurating in_a number of countries / a level of mass prosperity / which was undreamt_of / in the days preceding the Industrial Revolution. But, on the immediate impact_of the Industrial Revolution, there were substantial divergences among writers.

104: Diversity of Language 
        
            
        
        
The diversity of human language / may be compared to the diversity of the natural world. Just_as the demise of plant species / reduces genetic diversity, and deprives humanity / or potential medical and biological resources. So extinction_of language takes with_it / a wealth_of culture, art and knowledge.

106: Vanilla 
        
            
        
        
The uniquely scented flavor of vanilla / is second_only to chocolate in popularity / on the world’s palate. It’s also the second most expensive spice after saffron. But highly labor intensive cultivation methods / and the plant’s temperamental life cycle / and propagation / mean production on_a global scale is struggling to keep_up / with the increasing demand for the product.

108: Teacher’s instruction
In classes, your teachers will talk about topics that you are studying. The information that they provide will be important to know when you take tests. You must be able to take good written notes from what your teacher says.

110: Private Equity 
        
            
        
        
It_isn’t rare for private equity houses to hire grads / fresh_out_of business school, he said, but 9 times out_of 10, the students who nab these jobs / are the ones who had private equity experience under their belt / before even starting their MBA program.

111: No Ordinary Book
        
            
        
        
This book_is no ordinary book, and should not be read through / from beginning to end. It contains many different adventures, and the path you take / will depend_on the choices you make along the way. The success or failure of your mission / will depend_on the decisions you make, so think carefully before choosing.

113: Legal Writing 
        
            
        
        
Legal writing is usually less discursive / than writing in other humanities subjects, and precision is more important than variety. Sentence structure should not be too complex; it_is usually unnecessary / to make extensive use_of adjectives / or adverbs, and consistency of terms is often required.

114: Semiconductor 
        
            
        
        
The semiconductor industry / has been_able to improve the performance_of electronic systems / for more than four decades / by making ever-smaller devices. However, this_approach will soon encounter both scientific / and technical limits, which_is why the industry is exploring / a number of alternative device technologies.

115: Two Sisters 
        
            
        
        
Two sisters were at_a dinner party / when the conversation turned to upbringing. The elder sister started to say that / her parents had been very strict / and that she had been rather frightened_of them. Her sister, younger by two years, interrupted in_amazement. “What_are you talking about?” she said. “Our parents were very lenient.”

116: Weakness 
        
            
        
        
Weakness in electronics, auto / and gas station sales / dragged down overall retail sales last month, but excluding those three categories, retailers enjoyed healthy increases across the board, according to government figures released Wednesday. Moreover, December sales numbers were also revised higher.

117: Japanese Tea Ceremony 
05/Jun/Mel
        
            
        
        
The Japanese tea ceremony / is_a ritual tour influenced by Buddhism / in which green tea is prepared_and served / to a small group_of guests / in_a peaceful setting. The ceremony can take_as long as four hours / and there are many traditional gestures / that both the server and the guest must perform.

119: Russia 
        
            
        
        
Long isolated from Western Europe, Russia grew up without participating / in the develodment like the Reformation / that many Russians taking pride_in their unique culture / find dubious value. Russia is, as_a result, themos tunusual_ member of the European family, if_indeed_it_is European at_all. The question is still open to debate, particularly among Russians themselves.

120: Marketing Management 
        
            
        
        
For any marketing course that requires development_of marketing plan, such_as marketing management, marketing strategy / and segmentation support marketing, this_is the only planning handbook / that guides students through the step by step creation_of customized marketing plan, while offering commercial software to aid_in the process.

121: 21st century 
        
            
        
        
The beginning of the twenty-first century / will be remembered, not for military conflicts / or political events, but for a whole new age_of globalization - a ‘flattening’ of the world. The explosion of advanced technologies / now means that / suddenly knowledge pools_and resources / have connected all over the planet, levelling the playing field as never before.

122: Orientalists 
        
            
        
        
Orientalists, like many other nineteenth-century thinkers, conceive_of humanity / either in large collective terms / or in_abstract generalities. Orientalists are neither interested_in / nor capable_of discussing individuals; instead, artificial entities predominate. Similarly, the age-old distinction between “Europe” and “Asia” / or “Occident” and “Orient“ /herds beneath very wide labels / every possible variety of human plurality, reducing it in the process / to one or two terminal collective realities.

123: Fast Food 
        
            
        
        
Hundreds_of  millions_of American people / eat fast food every day / without giving it too much thought, unaware of the subtle / and not so subtle ramifications_of their purchases. They just grab their tray off the counter, find_a table, take_a seat, unwrap the paper, and dig_in. The whole experience_is transitory / and soon forgotten.

125: Statistical Information 
        
            
        
        
The provision_of accurate / and authoritative statistical information / strengthens modern societies. It provides_a basis / for decisions to be made_on such things / as where to open schools and hospitals, how much money to spend_on welfare payments / and even which football players / to replace at half-time.

126: Electric Car 
        
            
        
        
First-year university students / have designed and built_a  groundbreaking electric car / that recharges itself. Fifty students from the University of Sydney’s Faculty of Engineering / spent five months / cobbling together bits_of plywood, foam_and fiberglass / to build the ManGo concept car. They developed the specifications / and hand built the car. It’s_a pretty radical design: a four-wheel drive / with_a motor in each wheel.

127: The Preparation of Abstract
        
            
        
        
The preparation_of_abstracts / is an_intellectual_effort, requiring general familiarity with the subject. To bring out the salient points of_an_author’s_argument / calls for skills_and_experience. Consequently, a considerable_amount_of qualified manpower / that could be used to advantage / in other ways must be diverted to the task / of facilitating access to information.

129: Shrimp 
        
            
        
        
Shrimp farmers used to hold_animals / in nursery ponds for 30 to 60 days; now they try to move them / into grow-out ponds / in less than 30 days. This reduces stress on the animals / and dramatically increases survivals / in the grow-out ponds. Many farms that abandoned nursery ponds / have gone back to them, and the results have been surprisingly positive. They’re using the old, uncovered, earthen, nursery ponds.

130: Environmental Policy Course 
        
            
        
        
Along with customary classes_on subjects / such_as finance, accounting, and marketing, today’s MBA students are enrolling on courses / for environmental policy and stewardship. Indeed, more than half_of business schools require a course / in_environmental sustainability / or corporate social responsibility, according to a survey / of 91 US business schools, published_in_October 2005.

131: Public Demand for Education 
        
            
        
        
Public demand for education has remained strong, reflecting the importance_of education / as_a means_of social progress. Aware of the social value of_education / to the world_of the work, the government continues to innovate / and update the education system / in_order to produce_a qualified_and competent work force.

132: Business School Admissions 
        
            
        
        
Business school admissions_officers said / the new drive to attract younger students / was_in part the result_of_a realization / that they had inadvertently limited their applicant pool / by requiring several years’ work experience. Talented students who might otherwise have gone to business school / instead opted for a law or policy degree / because they were intimidated / by the expectation of work experience.

133: Online Shopping Environments 
A unique characteristic of online shopping environments is that they allow vendors to create retail interfaces with highly interactive features. One desirable form of interactivity from a consumer perspective is the implementation of sophisticated tools to assist shoppers in their purchase decisions by customizing the electronic shopping environment to their individual preferences.

134: Hazard Assessment 
A Hazard Assessment should be performed for work involving distillations of organic liquids and should thoroughly address issues relating to residual water and possible decomposition of the solvent in question, as well as the physical placement of the distillation apparatus and heating equipment to be employed.

135: Bookkeepers 
        
            
        
        
+31 million stolen. Of the cases identified and nine instances involved men. However, male bookkeepers who defrauded their employer / stole three times, on average, the amount that women stole.

136: Domestic Work 
Traditional divisions of domestic work are understood to persist because of the strong association of the home with femininity and paid work with masculinity - to challenge who does what in the home is arguably equivalent to challenging what it is to be a woman or a man.

137: The Only Family 
        
            
        
        
Imagine living all your life / as the only family on your street. Then, one morning, you open the front door / and discover houses all_around you. You see neighbors tending their gardens / and children walking to school. Where did all the people come from? What_if the answer turned_out to be that / they had always been there — you just hadn’t seen them?

138: Heterogeneous Students 
To work effectively with the heterogeneous student populations found in our schools, educators have the responsibility of acknowledging the cultural backgrounds of their students and the cultural setting in which the school is located in order to develop effective instructional strategies.

139: The Most Measurable Benefit 
Perhaps the most measurable benefit of the program has been the opportunity to meet in small groups. Something that is difficult to arrange such a desperate organization. Many officers would have to work together for thirty years but would not know other’s strengths and weaknesses.

140: Teenage Girls 
Teenage girls are continuing to outperform boys in English while the gender gap in achievements in math and science has almost disappeared. The figures show that last year 80% of 14-year-old girls reached at least the expected level 5 in English, compared with 65% of boys. But in math, the girls are just 1% ahead of boys, while in science the difference is 2%.

141: Black Swan 
Before the discovery of Australia, people in the old world were convinced that all swans were white, an unassailable belief as it seemed completely confirmed by empirical evidence. The sighting of the first black swan might have been an interesting surprise for a few ornithologists, but that is not where the significance of the story lies.

142: Important Values of Literature 
        
            
        
        
Certainly / one_of the important values_of literature is that / it nourishes_our emotional lives. An_effective literary work / may seem to speak directly to us / especially if we are ripe for_it. The inner life / that good writers reveal in their characters / often gives_us glimpses / of some portion_of ourselves. We can be moved to laugh, cry, tremble, dream, ponder, shriek, or rage with_a character by simply turning the page / instead_of turning our lives upside down.

143: Internal Combustion Engine 
Internal combustion engine enables the driver to decide which source of power is appropriate for the travel requirements of a given journey. Major US auto manufacturers are now developing feasible hybrid electric vehicles, and some are exploring fuel-cell technology for their electric cars.

144: Being Green 
You used to think that being green was a luxury for your company, but climate change has made you realise that you can no longer ignore it. The buzz is about becoming carbon-neutral, but where do you start? Consider your drivers. Do you want to become carbon-neutral for marketing reasons, for financial reasons, or just to save the planet?

145: The Maximum Yield of Plants 
        
            
        
        
The maximum yield_of plants, determined by their genetic potential, is seldom achieved / because factors such_as insufficient water or nutrients, adverse climate conditions, plant diseases, and insect damage / will limit growth_at some stage. Plants subjected to these biotic and abiotic constraints / are said to be stressed.

146: A World Without Light
        
            
        
        
Have you ever pictured_a world without light? Just think / how much we rely on man-made light sources_in_our lives. Without engineers, we wouldn’t be able to live the way we do. No street lights, no TV, no computer display, no house lights. Engineers design_and build / all these things, and they also design, build and run the electricity systems / that power all these light sources.

147: Sustainable Agriculture 
        
            
        
        
Since_its_inception, the UN system has been working / to ensure adequa¡te™ foo¡d™ for all / through sustainable agriculture. The majority of the world’s poores¡t™ people / live_in rural areas_of developing countries. They depen d_on agriculture / and related activities for their livelihoods. This makes them particularly vulnerable / to man-made_an¡d™ natural influences / that reduce agricultural production.

148: Recycle 
When we recycle, used materials are converted into new products, reducing the need to consume natural resources. If used materials are not recycled, new products are made by extracting fresh, raw material from the Earth, through mining and forestry. Recycling helps conserve important raw materials and protects natural habitats for the future.

149: Language Appearance
        
            
        
        
I¡t™ seems that language appeare¡d™ from nowhere / since no other species has anything resembling human language. However, other animals do possess basic systems for perceiving / and producing sounds that enable them to communicate. The¡se™ systems may have been in place / before the appearance_of language.

 
151: Rural Population 
Thus, a country might possess a sizeable rural population, but have an economic system in which the interests of the voters were predominantly related to their incomes, not to their occupations or location; and in such a country the political system would be unlikely to include an important agrarian party.

152: Information Technology 
The emergence of information technology has created new products, processes and distribution systems. New products include the computer, the Internet and digital TV; new processes include Internet banking, automated inventory control and automated teller machines; and new distribution systems include cable and satellite TV.

153: Informative Speech 
The purpose of the informative speech is to provide interesting, useful, and unique information to your audience. By dedicating yourself to the goals of providing information and appealing to your audience, you can take a positive step toward succeeding in your efforts as an informative speaker.

154: Method of learning 
        
            
        
        
There_is no single method_of learning / that guarantees success. How we learn that / depends_on many different factors. And what works best for you / will not necessarily be the same_as the approach / used for the other students / even they study the same course. We are all unique as learners, although some patterns emerge / from any groups_of students.

155: Electronic Discourse 
Electronic discourse is one form of interactive electronic communication. In this study, we reserve the term for the two-directional texts in which one person using a keyboard writes language that appears on the sender’s monitor and is transmitted to the monitor of a recipient, who responds by a keyboard.

156: Magnetar 
The best comparison is likely a magnetar, a young neutron star with a powerful magnetic field, the researchers said. Magnetars also produce bright X-ray flares. While magnetars are thought to be young stars, the two flaring objects in this study reside near elliptical galaxies, which contain older stars. So the objects are likely too old to be magnetars, the researchers said.

157: Actor Training
The training of an actor is an intensive process which requires curiosity, courage and commitment. You will learn how to prepare for rehearsal, how to rehearse and how to use independent and proactive processes that inform you to do the best work possible for both stage and screen.

158: Modern Food Production 
Globalization has affected what we eat in ways we are only beginning to understand. Modern food production is no longer related to our biological needs but is in direct conflict with them. The relationship between diet and our fertility, our cancer, heart disease and mental illness is becoming clear. Yet much of our food is nutritionally bankrupt.

159: Examination Candidates 
The department determines whether or not the candidate has passed the examination. In cases where an appearance for the final public oral examination would constitute a substantial financial hardship for the candidate, the director of graduate studies, may recommend to the dean of the Graduate School virtual, videoconferenced examination of the candidate.

160: Companies 
Companies will want to be known not just for the financial results they generate, but equally for the imprint they leave on society as a whole. First, ensuring that their products contribute positively. Second, operating in a way that approaches a “net-neutral” impact on the natural environment. And third, cherishing their people.

161: Constellation
A constellation is an area on the celestial sphere in which a group of stars forms an imaginary outline or pattern, typically representing an animal, mythological person or creature, or an inanimate object. The origins of the earliest constellations likely go back to prehistory.

162: Global Financial Crisis
New research shows that during the global financial crisis, workers who stayed in jobs did not reduce their working hours, despite the claims that cuts in hours have led to job losses. A study found that the life of people who stayed with the same employer remained relatively unchanged.

079: Introvert and Extrovert 
        
            
        
        
Although introvert and extrovert personality types / differ from one another on various grounds, the major difference between the two / is their source_of rejuvenation. While for extroverts, this may mean interaction_or excursions / with friends and family, the same may mean reading a book / or listening to music for introverts.

entry.title: PTE流程&题型
entry.content:
PTE

Pearson Test of English Academic: 培生英语语言测试

考试 71 道小题， 180分钟。

模考

官方模考卷分为A,B,C,三套

ID难度实际考试相似度价格
A⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
B⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
C⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️$35.99
Platinum全部ABC$79.99

模考切记使用苹果Airpod或其他蓝牙耳机， 我两次模考打水飘就因为用了蓝牙耳机， 模糊， 不识别。


PTE 对应 IELTS 分数

SoEMark
PTE30364250586573798386
IELTS4.55.05.56.06.57.07.58.08.59.0

PTE保 6.5分 所需正确率

目标分数64分正确率重点复习每日

段落排序RP2 / 2⭐️

选词填空FIB5 / 5⭐️⭐️20
录音总结SST2 / 2⭐️⭐️8

听词填空FIB2 / 2⭐️⭐️8

补全对话SMW2 / 2⭐️
识别错词HIW2 / 2⭐️
听写句子WFD2 / 2⭐️⭐️⭐️50
